I actually think they should care more, and I'm working on that with them, but, you know, because they're eventually going to have to drive sales.
But, yeah, so largely, like, focused on content that's going to exist on your website.
Now, we usually wouldn't start with white papers early on.
And part of that isn't just because, like, it's not like we wouldn't want to do that.
It's like, is the white paper really going to

You know, we tend to think about it like, you know, what is that like next best action?
So you get these, you get the right people, you're getting engagements on the topics that we want.
What comes next?
What is that next practice action?
Sometimes brands will think of that as like, you know, quite basic, like a demo, talk to the sales team, whatever it may be.
I mean, of course, we all want more of that, but that doesn't mean everyone's going to be ready.
It may not even be ready for a white paper.
So, you know, we tend to try to experiment of like, what is that next best thing to keep them engaged?
Yeah, yeah, that makes a ton of sense.
So really focusing on just all that content on the website to build that EO SEO.
And then later on, there's opportunities, like if we wanted to swap out one a week for a different medium, we could do that.
I could partner with you on that.
I'm just trying to think of, like, as we grow, what the opportunities would be.
But it makes a lot of sense to start on the website, on the blog.
Yeah, we can always be nimble.
I mean, you know, is it we do this, we do that.

When it can, it tends to be that sort of flexibility, it tends to be more on, like, the topics we're focused on rather than, like, hey, this week we may do a white paper, but next week, like, those things often needs to be, like, planned in advance.
And there's a lot of coordination that goes into it.
That's not that we can't move fast, but, you know, so.
And then the other thing we have to kind of, like, consider, like, when it comes to back to your, the point you mentioned on newsletters of what does that look like?
Like, we're at a phase now where we're just at, like, specifically pricing and packaging for that.
And so that would very likely be, depending on complexity, like another 9 in 12K.
Because it truly is that much effort.

Because it's not only you create that content, but all that comes into, you know, I've been able to see a lot of it firsthand this week as we spun up Augment Code and just the team that's doing that and reviewing the metrics and making sure everything's dialed in and continuing to progress that.
Like, we're completely owning that for them.
They're just giving us feedback here and there.
Yeah, makes sense.
So, you know, tying it back to your assessment at the beginning, which was really helpful, and then what y'all can do.
Like, what's your point of view for, I higher level, what you'd say to focus on, which it sounds like long form on the blog, generating that foundation for SEO and GEO.
But, like, what do you think, like, as, you know, partnering together, like, what are the outcomes that you think that you could help us strive for the short term?
Is it just...
just...
So it's honestly hard for me to say.
And that was kind of like, I don't want to oversell, right?
Like, but there's some brands I talk to that I can just look at and very quickly be like, okay, like, based off of this, like, there's so much headroom, there's so many quick win opportunities.
It's not that I don't think that there's wins out here.
It's just they're not as going to be as obvious.
And so when it comes to answering that question more specifically, I would be lying if I said I have a good pulse on that at this point.

You know, I would typically think about it related back to the earlier parts of the discussion, you know, trying to understand and work back of, do we see signal where there is search volume behind us?
You know, whether that's some of the jobs we've done stuff or the many other things we had walked through of like how to work back and identify those patterns.
If we're struggling to continue to find it, doesn't mean that it's out there.
It just needs a bit of experimentation to find it.
And so I would almost argue typically when I would think about math bounds, I'm not sure we'd be the best.
But for you, if that is accurate today, but it could be in the field because maybe you do get more traction.
Like I would usually look at it with like, because of the way we structure our agreements, you know, it's a two month strategy sprint and then a 12 month engagement.
You know, that's like, that's why you can't opt out after the first, you know, eight weeks.
Are you going to have enough signal to be confident of opting into a 12 month at that point, at that price point?
And if I was in your shoes, I'm not sure I would have enough information to do that yet.
I would typically think about like, let's experiment first, figure out where we get traction.
And I want to caveat on this.
I could very well be like missing some really important like nuance and digging.
And there's more like fruitful opportunities.
think again, back to what Andrew had mentioned, but that was just, yeah, the perspective I found throughout the week.
Yeah, that's really helpful.
Um, yeah, I to give you a little bit more like this kind of our thinking on it.
So

I'm curious to hear your feedback after that, but we're kind of like in a blue ocean right now for us, this market, because AI knowledge management, there's a bunch of tools out there, but they're all white collar focused.
We're the only ones that are, I guess, ambitious enough to take on manufacturing because we're uniquely equipped to with with Andrew and then Tyson and the team.

So, like, not only do we understand the industry really well, and it's really hard for anybody to understand the industry unless you've lived in it, but, you we were able to build a tool that performs better for this industry, better than any other tool out there, because there's so much complexity with how you need to, you know, what.
Yeah, so, like, we have this blue ocean, which is great and really exciting.
Then on the flip side of that, I guess blue ocean, but we're also a new product to it.
Like, just, we kind of just found this like little passion ocean, right?
Just recently, we're trying to kind of get our bearings.
We just recently, like our website's a couple weeks old.
Our product is very fresh too.
So we're fresh to this new market.
And we just haven't quite, there's not like, we haven't put dimensions around it yet or really understood what that looks like, which is kind of like the downside of the blue ocean.
You know, you don't know what people are searching for because it's not, like it's not, it's not a category that's defined yet.
So while we have a really huge opportunity and we're uniquely equipped to go to market in it, just trying to figure out like, well, what is that?
To your point, you know, how do we, what are the, what are people searching for that need this solution?
And like, who are competitors positioning themselves?
It's hard to figure that out when you don't have any, like you don't have any direct ones.
So sure.
And, and it could be that, you know, they're not searching for more of the things that you,
You know, it would be low-hanging fruit for you, and that's not necessarily, like, that doesn't mean you can't drive growth and really, you know, find some great traction.
It's just more, to me, like, it informs, I think sometimes SEO is just thought about that it's going to be a fit for everyone.
And, you know, we're not just pure SEO, but that's usually where, like, if you're not getting some sort of SEO value, it's hard to justify our cost from an ROI perspective.

And so, so I'm sure some of that is out there, but, like, yeah, typically I would think about leaning into things, more social-based events, you know, putting up leadership across a variety of mediums that is going to engage that audience, you know, publications, things of that nature.
And then, you know, does that, and then, like, it's not that don't do SEO, don't get me wrong there, it's just more, like, how big of a bet do you make on it?
No doubt.
But I'm sure you all will have some sort of traction in this channel at some point or another.
I just tend to think about it of the order operations of when you place that bet.
Yeah.
Yeah, it makes a ton of sense.
Which, yeah, sometimes these things happen.
And for us, I have also been wrong many times in my career before.

But obviously, if there's an opportunity for us to partner one day, and if there's things we can follow up with, I'm more than happy to, again, my aim is just, we want to find, those are amazing for us, who also are like, are going to be pumped to work with us because of all the value we're bringing.

Yeah, that makes sense.
So are you thinking that it's a little bit too early and that we should stay in touch?
Is that weird?

But yes, you know, happy to stay in touch.
Sorry, my memory is failing me now.
I can't recall if I shared, I think I shared we have the workshop tomorrow, right?
So, you know, that could help inform a little bit more deeper of how we think and there could be some things that kind of come through that.
But yes, if there's an opportunity to dig in further and, you know, there was a lot of stuff we talked about at the top middle part of this call that I'm happy to explore deeper.
This isn't me just trying to push you all the way to be clear.
It's more just trying to ensure I'm, you know, making use of your time valuable.
Yeah, no, I'd appreciate it.
And yeah, again, I really appreciate your approach.
So one thing for the workshop tomorrow, is it possible to get a recording of that?
had a meeting pop up.
yeah, yeah.
We'll send that out.
I'm not sure exactly how soon after, but yeah, we'll absolutely make sure that's sent out.
Awesome.
I appreciate that.
I guess Andrew and I will just kind of debrief and figure out.
It's definitely, it seems like, I mean, really like what y'all are doing, and I think that this would be valuable when it's the right time.
guess we'll figure out if we think we want to just go with it now and kind of see where it takes us or if we need to do a little bit more prep work.
And then I guess we'll get back to you in the next few days, you know, by next week to figure out what the next steps would be, whether moving forward or just staying in touch.
Yeah, and if helpful, you know, to have a session where we dig a little bit deeper to validate opportunities and talk about what that looks like and dig into how we've done that, some similar things.
For customers, I'm happy to do that as well.
Yeah.
However, I can help inform to see what the opportunity looks like.
Yeah.
Really appreciate it.
Really appreciate it.
Well, thanks for taking the time, Kyle.
Yeah.
Likewise.
Appreciate you guys being flexible again on the time earlier this week.
But yeah, feel free to reach back out.

It's fine.

We'll have a great vacation.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Follow-up discussion to assess fit and explore potential content marketing collaboration between Circuit and GrowthX.](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=0.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [GrowthX typically focuses on SEO-driven content strategies, but Circuit's current market position may not be ideal for this approach yet](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=200.0)
  - [Circuit is in a "blue ocean" market for AI knowledge management in manufacturing, presenting unique opportunities and challenges](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2156.0)
  - [GrowthX recommends experimenting with thought leadership, social content, and events before making a large SEO investment](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2275.0)
  - [Both parties agree to stay in touch and potentially revisit collaboration when timing is more suitable](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2393.0)

## Topics

### Current Market Position and Challenges

  - [Circuit is uniquely positioned in AI knowledge management for manufacturing](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2156.0)
  - [Lack of direct competitors makes it challenging to benchmark and identify search trends](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2246.0)
  - [Website and product are very new, still defining market dimensions and audience needs](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2227.0)

### Content Strategy Considerations

  - [GrowthX typically recommends 5-10 long-form articles per week (9-15k/month)](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=1726.0)
  - [Focus on website content initially, with potential for newsletters and multimedia later](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=1557.0)
  - [Difficulty in identifying clear SEO opportunities for Circuit at this stage](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2043.0)
  - [Suggestion to experiment with thought leadership, social content, and events](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2275.0)

### Potential Use Cases and Topics

  - [AI for technicians / technician enablement](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=897.0)
  - [Improving interfaces for SAP and ERP systems](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=907.0)
  - [Configure Price Quote (CPQ) optimization for companies like Arrow Electronics](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=897.0)
  - [Addressing disconnection in manufacturing supply chains](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=875.0)

### GrowthX Capabilities and Approach

  - [Consultative approach, focusing on ROI and client success](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=820.0)
  - [Flexibility to adjust content types and strategies as needed](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=1942.0)
  - [Experience with category creation and thought leadership (e.g., Biologica project)](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=1836.0)
  - [Potential for future expansion into more comprehensive growth services](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=1660.0)

## Next Steps

  - [Circuit team to debrief and decide on moving forward or staying in touch](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2396.0)
  - [GrowthX to send recording of upcoming workshop](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2463.0)
  - [Potential for a deeper strategy session to validate opportunities if desired](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2508.0)
  - [Circuit to follow up within a week on next steps or future contact](https://fathom.video/share/syebZFGSznD3WmK1RRHznxsibBmzxts6?tab=summary&timestamp=2499.0)

action_items.description: Debrief w/ Andrew Peters re GrowthX engagement. Decide on next steps (proceed now vs more prep). Contact Kyle w/ decision by next week.
Debrief w/ Andrew Reese re GrowthX engagement. Decide on next steps (proceed now vs more prep). Ensure Kyle contacted w/ decision by next week.
